mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading above 4.5% signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in housing starts indicates a supply-demand mismatch where capacity constraints outpace consumer spending, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, an increase in the federal funds rate combined with rising consumer prices tightens financial conditions, reducing corporate leverage capacity and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when the real rate proxy rises during tightening, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% in a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, directly boosting equity cash flow projections and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with accelerating GDP growth are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 250,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, it signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 250k for 2 consecutive months AND UNRATE < 4.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the overheating labor signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while real retail sales remain elevated indicates inventory drawdowns and strong underlying demand, prompting firms to raise prices and expand margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when INDPRO and RSAFS move in opposite directions, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a rapid decline in the unemployment rate combined with stable federal funds rates signals successful disinflation without a recession, reducing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS unchanged
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when the unemployment rate falls rapidly alongside stable policy rates, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% in a 10-year out-of-sample test.
